805	  local -r z_bottle_vessel="rbev-bottle-ccyolo"
806	  local -r z_nameplate_file="${RBBC_dot_dir}/${z_moniker}/${RBCC_rbrn_file}"
807	  local -r z_ssh_tabtarget="tt/rbw-cS.SshTo.${z_moniker}.sh"
808	
809	  # Inline yelps (not worth kindle constants — track-specific)
810	  buyy_cmd_yawp "rbw-fk"
811	  local -r z_cmd_rbw_fk="${z_buym_yelp}"
812	  buyy_cmd_yawp "rbw-cKB"
813	  local -r z_cmd_rbw_cKB="${z_buym_yelp}"
814	  buyy_cmd_yawp "RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK="
815	  local -r z_cmd_sentry_hallmark="${z_buym_yelp}"
816	  buyy_cmd_yawp "${z_ssh_tabtarget}"
817	  local -r z_cmd_ssh="${z_buym_yelp}"
818	
819	  # --- Probes ---
820	
821	  # Docker runtime
822	  local z_has_docker=0
823	  command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1 && z_has_docker=1
824	
825	  # Nameplate exists
826	  local z_nameplate_exists=0
827	  test -f "${z_nameplate_file}" && z_nameplate_exists=1
828	
829	  # Hallmarks populated in nameplate
830	  local z_sentry_hallmark="" z_bottle_hallmark=""
831	  local z_sentry_hallmark_present=0 z_bottle_hallmark_present=0
832	  if test "${z_nameplate_exists}" = "1"; then
833	    z_sentry_hallmark=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${z_nameplate_file}" "RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK") || z_sentry_hallmark=""
834	    z_bottle_hallmark=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${z_nameplate_file}" "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK") || z_bottle_hallmark=""
835	    test -n "${z_sentry_hallmark}" && z_sentry_hallmark_present=1
836	    test -n "${z_bottle_hallmark}" && z_bottle_hallmark_present=1
837	  fi
838	
839	  # Kludge-tagged images exist locally (k-prefixed hallmarks)
840	  local z_sentry_image_exists=0 z_bottle_image_exists=0
841	  if test "${z_has_docker}" = "1"; then
842	    if docker images --format "{{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}}" 2>/dev/null \
843	       | grep -q "${z_sentry_vessel}:k[0-9]"; then
844	      z_sentry_image_exists=1
845	    fi
846	    if docker images --format "{{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}}" 2>/dev/null \
847	       | grep -q "${z_bottle_vessel}:k[0-9]"; then
848	      z_bottle_image_exists=1
849	    fi
850	  fi
851	
852	  # Crucible charged
853	  # [INFRA-NEEDED] Currently rbw-cic is a tabtarget (param1 channel).
854	  # For probe use, need a scriptable function that returns 0/1 without
855	  # running the full dispatch. For now, check docker containers directly.
856	  local z_crucible_charged=0
857	  if test "${z_has_docker}" = "1"; then
858	    if docker ps --format "{{.Names}}" 2>/dev/null \
859	       | grep -q "^${z_moniker}-bottle\$"; then
860	      z_crucible_charged=1
861	    fi
862	  fi
863	
864	  # --- Header ---
865	  buh_section "Start a Crucible Using Local Builds"
866	  buh_e
867	  buh_line "A ${RBYC_CRUCIBLE} is a sandboxed container environment with enforced"
868	  buh_t   "network isolation. You are going to build one on your workstation"
869	  buh_t   "and run Claude Code inside it — no cloud account, no credentials"
870	  buh_t   "beyond your own Claude subscription."
871	  buh_e
872	  buh_line "This track uses the ${RBYC_CCYOLO} ${RBYC_NAMEPLATE}: a Claude Code sandbox that can"
873	  buh_t   "only reach Anthropic. Everything else is blocked."
874	  buh_e
875	
876	  # Docker gate
877	  if test "${z_has_docker}" = "0"; then
878	    buh_E   "Docker is not available on this machine."
879	    buh_t   "Install Docker Desktop (or dockerd in WSL) and re-run this handbook."
880	    buh_e
881	    buh_tT  "Return to start: " "${RBZ_ONBOARD_START_HERE}"
882	    buh_e
883	    return 0
884	  fi
885	
886	  buh_t   "Prerequisite: a Claude OAuth subscription (you will authenticate"
887	  buh_t   "inside the container via copy/paste from your browser)."
888	  buh_e
889	
890	  buh_step_style "Step " " — "
891	
892	  # =================================================================
893	  # Step 1: Build images locally
894	  # =================================================================
895	  buh_step1 "Build images locally"
896	  buh_e
897	  buh_line "A ${RBYC_VESSEL} is a specification for a container image — a Dockerfile"
898	  buh_line "and build context. A ${RBYC_HALLMARK} is a specific build instance,"
899	  buh_t   "identified by a timestamp tag."
900	  buh_e
901	  buh_line "${RBYC_KLUDGE} builds a vessel image locally using Docker — no cloud, no"
902	  buh_t   "registry, no credentials. The fastest path from Dockerfile to running"
903	  buh_t   "container without cloud image backup."
904	  buh_e
905	
906	  # --- Substep 1a: Kludge the sentry ---
907	  buh_step2 "Kludge the sentry"
908	  buh_e
909	  buh_line "The ${RBYC_SENTRY} is the gatekeeper container. It runs iptables"
910	  buh_t   "and dnsmasq to enforce network policy — only domains you whitelist"
911	  buh_t   "are reachable from inside."
912	  buh_e
913	  buh_t   "Kludge it:"
914	  buh_e
915	  buh_tTc "   " "${RBZ_KLUDGE_VESSEL}" " ${z_sentry_vessel}"
916	  buh_e
917	  buh_t   "The output prints a hallmark tag — something like"
918	  buh_tc  "" "k260413155458-f9dcb6d9"
919	  buh_t   "Copy it. You will paste it into the nameplate in Step 2."
920	  buh_e
921	
922	  # Sentry kludge image probe
923	  if test "${z_sentry_image_exists}" = "1"; then
924	    buh_ct  " [*] " "Kludge-tagged sentry image found locally"
925	  else
926	    buh_t   " [ ] No kludge-tagged sentry image found"
927	  fi
928	  buh_e
929	
930	  buh_tW  "" "Kludge requires a clean git tree."
931	  buh_t   "If you have uncommitted changes, commit them first.  This is by"
932	  buh_t   "design — container images not backed by git commits easy to confuse."
933	  buh_e
934	
935	  # --- Substep 1b: Kludge the bottle ---
936	  buh_step2 "Kludge the bottle"
937	  buh_e
938	  buh_line "The ${RBYC_BOTTLE} is your workload container — where Claude Code"
939	  buh_line "runs. The ${RBYC_CCYOLO} bottle includes SSH, node, and the Claude CLI."
940	  buh_e
941	  buh_t   "This command is different from the sentry kludge. It builds the"
942	  buh_line "bottle AND drives the ${RBYC_HALLMARK} into the nameplate"
943	  buh_t   "automatically:"
944	  buh_e
945	  buh_tTc "   " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_KLUDGE_BOTTLE}" " ${z_moniker}"
946	  buh_e
947	  buh_t   "Two kludge commands, two different jobs:"
948	  buh_line "  ${z_cmd_rbw_fk}  — standalone vessel kludge. Produces a hallmark, touches nothing else."
949	  buh_line "  ${z_cmd_rbw_cKB} — nameplate-aware bottle kludge. Builds AND installs the hallmark."
950	  buh_e
951	  buh_t   "The sentry is shared across nameplates, so you kludge it standalone"
952	  buh_t   "and drive the hallmark yourself. The bottle belongs to one nameplate,"
953	  buh_t   "so the tool does the driving for you."
954	  buh_e
955	
956	  # Bottle kludge image probe
957	  if test "${z_bottle_image_exists}" = "1"; then
958	    buh_ct  " [*] " "Kludge-tagged bottle image found locally"
959	  else
960	    buh_t   " [ ] No kludge-tagged bottle image found"
961	  fi
962	  buh_e
963	
964	  # =================================================================
965	  # Step 2: Drive sentry hallmark into the nameplate
966	  # =================================================================
967	  buh_step1 "Drive sentry hallmark into the nameplate"
968	  buh_e
969	  buh_line "A ${RBYC_NAMEPLATE} is the file that defines a Crucible — it specifies"
970	  buh_line "what ${RBYC_HALLMARK} to use for ${RBYC_SENTRY}, ${RBYC_PENTACLE}, and"
971	  buh_line "${RBYC_BOTTLE} containers. It lives at:"
972	  buh_e
973	  buh_c   "   ${z_nameplate_file}"
974	  buh_e
975	  buh_line "The ${RBYC_RBRN} file has two hallmark fields — one for the sentry"
976	  buh_t   "and one for the bottle. The bottle kludge (Step 1.2) already drove"
977	  buh_t   "its hallmark. Now you drive the sentry hallmark by hand."
978	  buh_e
979	  buh_t   "Open the nameplate file and paste the sentry hallmark from Step 1.1"
980	  buh_line "into the ${z_cmd_sentry_hallmark} field."
981	  buh_e
982	  buh_line "This is deliberately manual — it teaches you what ${RBYC_HALLMARKS} are and"
983	  buh_line "where they live. You just saw the automated version with the ${RBYC_BOTTLE};"
984	  buh_t   "now you see the mechanism underneath."
985	  buh_e
986	
987	  # Hallmark probes
988	  if test "${z_sentry_hallmark_present}" = "1"; then
989	    buh_tct " [*] " "RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK" " = ${z_sentry_hallmark}"
990	  else
991	    buh_tE  " [ ] " "RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK is empty — paste the sentry hallmark from Step 1.1"
992	  fi
993	  if test "${z_bottle_hallmark_present}" = "1"; then
994	    buh_tct " [*] " "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK" " = ${z_bottle_hallmark}"
995	  else
996	    buh_tE  " [ ] " "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK is empty — run Step 1.2 (bottle kludge)"
997	  fi
998	  buh_e
999	
1000	  buh_line "After editing, commit the change — the next ${RBYC_KLUDGE} will need a"
1001	  buh_t   "clean tree:"
1002	  buh_e
1003	  buh_c   "   git add ${z_nameplate_file}"
1004	  buh_c   "   git commit -m \"Drive sentry hallmark into ${z_moniker} nameplate\""
1005	  buh_e
1006	
1007	  # =================================================================
1008	  # Step 3: Charge the crucible
1009	  # =================================================================
1010	  buh_step1 "Charge the crucible"
1011	  buh_e
1012	  buh_line "${RBYC_CHARGE} starts three containers from your nameplate:"
1013	  buh_e
1014	  buh_line "  ${RBYC_SENTRY}   — runs iptables + dnsmasq, enforces the network allowlist"
1015	  buh_line "  ${RBYC_PENTACLE}  — establishes the network namespace shared with the bottle"
1016	  buh_line "  ${RBYC_BOTTLE}    — your workload container (Claude Code lives here)"
1017	  buh_e
1018	  buh_t   "The sentry mediates all traffic. The bottle never touches the"
1019	  buh_t   "network directly — everything routes through the sentry's rules."
1020	  buh_e
1021	  buh_tI  "   " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_CHARGE}" "${z_moniker}"
1022	  buh_e
1023	  buh_t   "Charge takes 10-30 seconds. It pulls the images from your local"
1024	  buh_t   "Docker, creates the enclave network, starts the containers, waits"
1025	  buh_t   "for the sentry to confirm its iptables rules are applied, then"
1026	  buh_t   "starts the bottle."
1027	  buh_e
1028	
1029	  # Charged probe
1030	  if test "${z_crucible_charged}" = "1"; then
1031	    buh_ct  " [*] " "Crucible ${z_moniker} is charged (bottle container running)"
1032	  else
1033	    buh_t   " [ ] Crucible ${z_moniker} is not charged"
1034	  fi
1035	  buh_e
1036	
1037	  # =================================================================
1038	  # Step 4: Enter the container and run Claude Code
1039	  # =================================================================
1040	  buh_step1 "Enter the container and run Claude Code"
1041	  buh_e
1042	  buh_t   "SSH into the bottle:"
1043	  buh_e
1044	  buh_line "   ${z_cmd_ssh}"
1045	  buh_e
1046	  buh_t   "You land as the claude user in ~/workspace, which contains"
1047	  buh_t   "a small sample project. Run Claude Code:"
1048	  buh_e
1049	  buh_c   "   claude"
1050	  buh_e
1051	  buh_t   "Claude Code will prompt you to authenticate. It opens a URL —"
1052	  buh_t   "copy it to your workstation browser, sign in with your Claude"
1053	  buh_t   "subscription, and paste the code back into the terminal."
1054	  buh_e
1055	  buh_tW  "" "The ccyolo bottle pins Claude Code to a specific version."
1056	  buh_t   "Versions after v2.1.89 have a regression where paste does not"
1057	  buh_t   "work in the OAuth input prompt through SSH or docker exec"
1058	  buh_t   "(github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/47745). If you"
1059	  buh_t   "update the pin and paste stops working, type the code manually."
1060	  buh_e
1061	  buh_t   "Once authenticated, Claude Code starts in full autonomy mode —"
1062	  buh_t   "no permission prompts. Inside a network-contained crucible,"
1063	  buh_t   "this is the correct posture: the sentry enforces the real"
1064	  buh_t   "security boundary, not the tool permission system."
1065	  buh_e
1066	  buh_t   "Try your first interaction:"
1067	  buh_e
1068	  buh_c   "   The count_words.sh script has bugs — can you find and fix them?"
1069	  buh_e
1070	  buh_t   "Watch Claude read the files, identify issues, and edit the code."
1071	  buh_t   "The workspace persists across charge/quench cycles — your changes"
1072	  buh_t   "survive restarts."
1073	  buh_e
1074	  buh_t   "Why SSH instead of docker exec?"
1075	  buh_e
1076	  buh_t   "Docker exec is laggy and breaks terminal resize — Claude Code's"
1077	  buh_t   "interactive display needs correct dimensions. SSH gives a proper"
1078	  buh_t   "login session with full terminal negotiation."
1079	  buh_e
1080	  buh_line "If SSH fails, ${RBYC_RACK} is the diagnostic fallback —"
1081	  buh_t   "docker exec into the bottle to inspect state:"
1082	  buh_e
1083	  buh_tTc "   " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_RACK}" " ${z_moniker}"
1084	  buh_e
1085	
1086	  # =================================================================
1087	  # Step 5: Verify network containment
1088	  # =================================================================
1089	  buh_step1 "Verify network containment"
1090	  buh_e
1091	  buh_line "From inside the ${RBYC_BOTTLE} (while SSH'd in), you can test what's reachable."
1092	  buh_line "The ${RBYC_CCYOLO} nameplate allows Anthropic and example.com (a test"
1093	  buh_t   "target). Everything else is blocked."
1094	  buh_e
1095	  buh_t   "Run these curl commands inside the bottle:"
1096	  buh_e
1097	  buh_c   "   curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' https://api.anthropic.com"

file.content:
rbho_first_crucible() {
  buc_doc_brief "Start a Crucible using local builds — kludge, charge, SSH, verify containment"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  # Hardcoded for this track — ccyolo is the teaching nameplate
  local -r z_moniker="ccyolo"
  local -r z_sentry_vessel="rbev-sentry-deb-tether"
  local -r z_bottle_vessel="rbev-bottle-ccyolo"
  local -r z_nameplate_file="${RBBC_dot_dir}/${z_moniker}/${RBCC_rbrn_file}"
  local -r z_ssh_tabtarget="tt/rbw-cS.SshTo.${z_moniker}.sh"

  # Inline yelps (not worth kindle constants — track-specific)
  buyy_cmd_yawp "rbw-fk"
  local -r z_cmd_rbw_fk="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_cmd_yawp "rbw-cKB"
  local -r z_cmd_rbw_cKB="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_cmd_yawp "RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK="
  local -r z_cmd_sentry_hallmark="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_cmd_yawp "${z_ssh_tabtarget}"
  local -r z_cmd_ssh="${z_buym_yelp}"

  # --- Probes ---

  # Docker runtime
  local z_has_docker=0
  command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1 && z_has_docker=1

  # Nameplate exists
  local z_nameplate_exists=0
  test -f "${z_nameplate_file}" && z_nameplate_exists=1

  # Hallmarks populated in nameplate
  local z_sentry_hallmark="" z_bottle_hallmark=""
  local z_sentry_hallmark_present=0 z_bottle_hallmark_present=0
  if test "${z_nameplate_exists}" = "1"; then
    z_sentry_hallmark=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${z_nameplate_file}" "RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK") || z_sentry_hallmark=""
    z_bottle_hallmark=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${z_nameplate_file}" "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK") || z_bottle_hallmark=""
    test -n "${z_sentry_hallmark}" && z_sentry_hallmark_present=1
    test -n "${z_bottle_hallmark}" && z_bottle_hallmark_present=1
  fi

  # Kludge-tagged images exist locally (k-prefixed hallmarks)
  local z_sentry_image_exists=0 z_bottle_image_exists=0
  if test "${z_has_docker}" = "1"; then
    if docker images --format "{{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}}" 2>/dev/null \
       | grep -q "${z_sentry_vessel}:k[0-9]"; then
      z_sentry_image_exists=1
    fi
    if docker images --format "{{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}}" 2>/dev/null \
       | grep -q "${z_bottle_vessel}:k[0-9]"; then
      z_bottle_image_exists=1
    fi
  fi

  # Crucible charged
  # [INFRA-NEEDED] Currently rbw-cic is a tabtarget (param1 channel).
  # For probe use, need a scriptable function that returns 0/1 without
  # running the full dispatch. For now, check docker containers directly.
  local z_crucible_charged=0
  if test "${z_has_docker}" = "1"; then
    if docker ps --format "{{.Names}}" 2>/dev/null \
       | grep -q "^${z_moniker}-bottle\$"; then
      z_crucible_charged=1
    fi
  fi

  # --- Header ---
  buh_section "Start a Crucible Using Local Builds"
  buh_e
  buh_line "A ${RBYC_CRUCIBLE} is a sandboxed container environment with enforced"
  buh_t   "network isolation. You are going to build one on your workstation"
  buh_t   "and run Claude Code inside it — no cloud account, no credentials"
  buh_t   "beyond your own Claude subscription."
  buh_e
  buh_line "This track uses the ${RBYC_CCYOLO} ${RBYC_NAMEPLATE}: a Claude Code sandbox that can"
  buh_t   "only reach Anthropic. Everything else is blocked."
  buh_e

  # Docker gate
  if test "${z_has_docker}" = "0"; then
    buh_E   "Docker is not available on this machine."
    buh_t   "Install Docker Desktop (or dockerd in WSL) and re-run this handbook."
    buh_e
    buh_tT  "Return to start: " "${RBZ_ONBOARD_START_HERE}"
    buh_e
    return 0
  fi

  buh_t   "Prerequisite: a Claude OAuth subscription (you will authenticate"
  buh_t   "inside the container via copy/paste from your browser)."
  buh_e

  buh_step_style "Step " " — "

  # =================================================================
  # Step 1: Build images locally
  # =================================================================
  buh_step1 "Build images locally"
  buh_e
  buh_line "A ${RBYC_VESSEL} is a specification for a container image — a Dockerfile"
  buh_line "and build context. A ${RBYC_HALLMARK} is a specific build instance,"
  buh_t   "identified by a timestamp tag."
  buh_e
  buh_line "${RBYC_KLUDGE} builds a vessel image locally using Docker — no cloud, no"
  buh_t   "registry, no credentials. The fastest path from Dockerfile to running"
  buh_t   "container without cloud image backup."
  buh_e

  # --- Substep 1a: Kludge the sentry ---
  buh_step2 "Kludge the sentry"
  buh_e
  buh_line "The ${RBYC_SENTRY} is the gatekeeper container. It runs iptables"
  buh_t   "and dnsmasq to enforce network policy — only domains you whitelist"
  buh_t   "are reachable from inside."
  buh_e
  buh_t   "Kludge it:"
  buh_e
  buh_tTc "   " "${RBZ_KLUDGE_VESSEL}" " ${z_sentry_vessel}"
  buh_e
  buh_t   "The output prints a hallmark tag — something like"
  buh_tc  "" "k260413155458-f9dcb6d9"
  buh_t   "Copy it. You will paste it into the nameplate in Step 2."
  buh_e

  # Sentry kludge image probe
  if test "${z_sentry_image_exists}" = "1"; then
    buh_ct  " [*] " "Kludge-tagged sentry image found locally"
  else
    buh_t   " [ ] No kludge-tagged sentry image found"
  fi
  buh_e

  buh_tW  "" "Kludge requires a clean git tree."
  buh_t   "If you have uncommitted changes, commit them first.  This is by"
  buh_t   "design — container images not backed by git commits easy to confuse."
  buh_e

  # --- Substep 1b: Kludge the bottle ---
  buh_step2 "Kludge the bottle"
  buh_e
  buh_line "The ${RBYC_BOTTLE} is your workload container — where Claude Code"
  buh_line "runs. The ${RBYC_CCYOLO} bottle includes SSH, node, and the Claude CLI."
  buh_e
  buh_t   "This command is different from the sentry kludge. It builds the"
  buh_line "bottle AND drives the ${RBYC_HALLMARK} into the nameplate"
  buh_t   "automatically:"
  buh_e
  buh_tTc "   " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_KLUDGE_BOTTLE}" " ${z_moniker}"
  buh_e
  buh_t   "Two kludge commands, two different jobs:"
  buh_line "  ${z_cmd_rbw_fk}  — standalone vessel kludge. Produces a hallmark, touches nothing else."
  buh_line "  ${z_cmd_rbw_cKB} — nameplate-aware bottle kludge. Builds AND installs the hallmark."
  buh_e
  buh_t   "The sentry is shared across nameplates, so you kludge it standalone"
  buh_t   "and drive the hallmark yourself. The bottle belongs to one nameplate,"
  buh_t   "so the tool does the driving for you."
  buh_e

  # Bottle kludge image probe
  if test "${z_bottle_image_exists}" = "1"; then
    buh_ct  " [*] " "Kludge-tagged bottle image found locally"
  else
    buh_t   " [ ] No kludge-tagged bottle image found"
  fi
  buh_e

  # =================================================================
  # Step 2: Drive sentry hallmark into the nameplate
  # =================================================================
  buh_step1 "Drive sentry hallmark into the nameplate"
  buh_e
  buh_line "A ${RBYC_NAMEPLATE} is the file that defines a Crucible — it specifies"
  buh_line "what ${RBYC_HALLMARK} to use for ${RBYC_SENTRY}, ${RBYC_PENTACLE}, and"
  buh_line "${RBYC_BOTTLE} containers. It lives at:"
  buh_e
  buh_c   "   ${z_nameplate_file}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "The ${RBYC_RBRN} file has two hallmark fields — one for the sentry"
  buh_t   "and one for the bottle. The bottle kludge (Step 1.2) already drove"
  buh_t   "its hallmark. Now you drive the sentry hallmark by hand."
  buh_e
  buh_t   "Open the nameplate file and paste the sentry hallmark from Step 1.1"
  buh_line "into the ${z_cmd_sentry_hallmark} field."
  buh_e
  buh_line "This is deliberately manual — it teaches you what ${RBYC_HALLMARKS} are and"
  buh_line "where they live. You just saw the automated version with the ${RBYC_BOTTLE};"
  buh_t   "now you see the mechanism underneath."
  buh_e

  # Hallmark probes
  if test "${z_sentry_hallmark_present}" = "1"; then
    buh_tct " [*] " "RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK" " = ${z_sentry_hallmark}"
  else
    buh_tE  " [ ] " "RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK is empty — paste the sentry hallmark from Step 1.1"
  fi
  if test "${z_bottle_hallmark_present}" = "1"; then
    buh_tct " [*] " "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK" " = ${z_bottle_hallmark}"
  else
    buh_tE  " [ ] " "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK is empty — run Step 1.2 (bottle kludge)"
  fi
  buh_e

  buh_line "After editing, commit the change — the next ${RBYC_KLUDGE} will need a"
  buh_t   "clean tree:"
  buh_e
  buh_c   "   git add ${z_nameplate_file}"
  buh_c   "   git commit -m \"Drive sentry hallmark into ${z_moniker} nameplate\""
  buh_e

  # =================================================================
  # Step 3: Charge the crucible
  # =================================================================
  buh_step1 "Charge the crucible"
  buh_e
  buh_line "${RBYC_CHARGE} starts three containers from your nameplate:"
  buh_e
  buh_line "  ${RBYC_SENTRY}   — runs iptables + dnsmasq, enforces the network allowlist"
  buh_line "  ${RBYC_PENTACLE}  — establishes the network namespace shared with the bottle"
  buh_line "  ${RBYC_BOTTLE}    — your workload container (Claude Code lives here)"
  buh_e
  buh_t   "The sentry mediates all traffic. The bottle never touches the"
  buh_t   "network directly — everything routes through the sentry's rules."
  buh_e
  buh_tI  "   " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_CHARGE}" "${z_moniker}"
  buh_e
  buh_t   "Charge takes 10-30 seconds. It pulls the images from your local"
  buh_t   "Docker, creates the enclave network, starts the containers, waits"
  buh_t   "for the sentry to confirm its iptables rules are applied, then"
  buh_t   "starts the bottle."
  buh_e

  # Charged probe
  if test "${z_crucible_charged}" = "1"; then
    buh_ct  " [*] " "Crucible ${z_moniker} is charged (bottle container running)"
  else
    buh_t   " [ ] Crucible ${z_moniker} is not charged"
  fi
  buh_e

  # =================================================================
  # Step 4: Enter the container and run Claude Code
  # =================================================================
  buh_step1 "Enter the container and run Claude Code"
  buh_e
  buh_t   "SSH into the bottle:"
  buh_e
  buh_line "   ${z_cmd_ssh}"
  buh_e
  buh_t   "You land as the claude user in ~/workspace, which contains"
  buh_t   "a small sample project. Run Claude Code:"
  buh_e
  buh_c   "   claude"
  buh_e
  buh_t   "Claude Code will prompt you to authenticate. It opens a URL —"
  buh_t   "copy it to your workstation browser, sign in with your Claude"
  buh_t   "subscription, and paste the code back into the terminal."
  buh_e
  buh_tW  "" "The ccyolo bottle pins Claude Code to a specific version."
  buh_t   "Versions after v2.1.89 have a regression where paste does not"
  buh_t   "work in the OAuth input prompt through SSH or docker exec"
  buh_t   "(github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/47745). If you"
  buh_t   "update the pin and paste stops working, type the code manually."
  buh_e
  buh_t   "Once authenticated, Claude Code starts in full autonomy mode —"
  buh_t   "no permission prompts. Inside a network-contained crucible,"
  buh_t   "this is the correct posture: the sentry enforces the real"
  buh_t   "security boundary, not the tool permission system."
  buh_e
  buh_t   "Try your first interaction:"
  buh_e
  buh_c   "   The count_words.sh script has bugs — can you find and fix them?"
  buh_e
  buh_t   "Watch Claude read the files, identify issues, and edit the code."
  buh_t   "The workspace persists across charge/quench cycles — your changes"
  buh_t   "survive restarts."
  buh_e
  buh_t   "Why SSH instead of docker exec?"
  buh_e
  buh_t   "Docker exec is laggy and breaks terminal resize — Claude Code's"
  buh_t   "interactive display needs correct dimensions. SSH gives a proper"
  buh_t   "login session with full terminal negotiation."
  buh_e
  buh_line "If SSH fails, ${RBYC_RACK} is the diagnostic fallback —"
  buh_t   "docker exec into the bottle to inspect state:"
  buh_e
  buh_tTc "   " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_RACK}" " ${z_moniker}"
  buh_e

  # =================================================================
  # Step 5: Verify network containment
  # =================================================================
  buh_step1 "Verify network containment"
  buh_e
  buh_line "From inside the ${RBYC_BOTTLE} (while SSH'd in), you can test what's reachable."
  buh_line "The ${RBYC_CCYOLO} nameplate allows Anthropic and example.com (a test"
  buh_t   "target). Everything else is blocked."
  buh_e
  buh_t   "Run these curl commands inside the bottle:"
  buh_e
  buh_c   "   curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' https://api.anthropic.com"

# Teaches: full conjure lifecycle from reliquary through cleanup.

rbho_director_first_build() {
  buc_doc_brief "Your First Cloud Build — inscribe, conjure, tour, summon, abjure"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  local -r z_vessel="rbev-sentry-deb-tether"

  # --- Probes ---

  # Director credential present
  local z_has_director=0 z_secrets_dir=""
  if test -f "${RBBC_rbrr_file}"; then
    z_secrets_dir=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${RBBC_rbrr_file}" "RBRR_SECRETS_DIR") || z_secrets_dir=""
  fi
  if test -n "${z_secrets_dir}" && \
     test -f "${z_secrets_dir}/${RBCC_role_director}/${RBCC_rbra_file}"; then
    z_has_director=1
  fi

  # Depot configured

   this is the correct posture: the sentry enforces the real

   [36m   curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' https://example.com[0m
      Expected: 200 (test target on allowlist)

   [36m   curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' --max-time 5 https://google.com[0m

   for this verification step — it proves the allowlist works

   [0m[34;4m]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Quench\Quench]8;;\[0m stops and removes all three containers and the[0m

   identified by a timestamp tag.

   registry, no credentials. The fastest path from Dockerfile to running
   container without cloud image backup.

0	#!/bin/bash
1	#
2	# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
3	#
4	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
5	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
6	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
7	#
8	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
9	#
10	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
11	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
12	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
13	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
14	# limitations under the License.
15	#
16	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
17	#
18	# Bash Utility Handbook - Always-visible user interaction output
19	#
20	# This module provides formatted output for interactive procedures where
21	# the user MUST see the output to proceed (OAuth flows, manual steps, etc).
22	# All output goes to stderr and is NOT subject to verbosity control.
23	#
24	# Function naming convention encodes color sequence for positional args:
25	#   t = text (default color)
26	#   c = command/code (cyan)
27	#   u = UI element (magenta) - text user sees on screen
28	#   W = warning (yellow) - uppercase for visual pop
29	#   E = error (red) - uppercase for visual pop
30	#   l = link (blue underline, OSC-8 hyperlink) - consumes TWO args
31	#
32	# Each letter consumes one positional argument, except 'l' which consumes
33	# two (display_text then url). This is the only multi-arg letter.
34	#
35	# Example: buh_tut "Click the " "Save" " button"
36	#   Renders: "Click the " + magenta("Save") + " button"
37	#
38	# Link arg mapping example for buh_tlt:
39	#   buh_tlt  "A "  "hallmark"  "https://...#hallmark"  " is a named artifact."
40	#            ^^^^  ^^^^^^^^^^  ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^   ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
41	#            t(1)  l(1:text)   l(2:url)                t(1)
42	
43	set -euo pipefail
44	
45	# Multiple inclusion guard
46	test -z "${ZBUH_INCLUDED:-}" || return 0
47	ZBUH_INCLUDED=1
48	
49	######################################################################
50	# Internal: Kindle and Sentinel
51	
52	zbuh_kindle() {
53	  test -z "${ZBUH_KINDLED:-}" || return 0
54	
55	  # Color support detection
56	  local z_use_color=0
57	  if test -z "${NO_COLOR:-}" && test -n "${TERM:-}" && test "${TERM}" != "dumb"; then
58	    z_use_color=1
59	  fi
60	
61	  if test "${z_use_color}" = "1"; then
62	    readonly ZBUH_R="\033[0m"          # Reset
63	    readonly ZBUH_T=""                 # Text (default - no color change)
64	    readonly ZBUH_C="\033[36m"         # Command (cyan)
65	    readonly ZBUH_U="\033[35m"         # UI element (magenta)
66	    readonly ZBUH_W="\033[1;33m"       # Warning (bright yellow)
67	    readonly ZBUH_E="\033[1;31m"       # Error (bright red)
68	    readonly ZBUH_S="\033[1;37m"       # Section (bright white)
69	    readonly ZBUH_L="\033[34;4m"       # Link (blue + underline)
70	  else
71	    readonly ZBUH_R=""
72	    readonly ZBUH_T=""
73	    readonly ZBUH_C=""
74	    readonly ZBUH_U=""
75	    readonly ZBUH_W=""
76	    readonly ZBUH_E=""
77	    readonly ZBUH_S=""

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Bash Utility Handbook - Always-visible user interaction output
#
# This module provides formatted output for interactive procedures where
# the user MUST see the output to proceed (OAuth flows, manual steps, etc).
# All output goes to stderr and is NOT subject to verbosity control.
#
# Function naming convention encodes color sequence for positional args:
#   t = text (default color)
#   c = command/code (cyan)
#   u = UI element (magenta) - text user sees on screen
#   W = warning (yellow) - uppercase for visual pop
#   E = error (red) - uppercase for visual pop
#   l = link (blue underline, OSC-8 hyperlink) - consumes TWO args
#
# Each letter consumes one positional argument, except 'l' which consumes
# two (display_text then url). This is the only multi-arg letter.
#
# Example: buh_tut "Click the " "Save" " button"
#   Renders: "Click the " + magenta("Save") + " button"
#
# Link arg mapping example for buh_tlt:
#   buh_tlt  "A "  "hallmark"  "https://...#hallmark"  " is a named artifact."
#            ^^^^  ^^^^^^^^^^  ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^   ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
#            t(1)  l(1:text)   l(2:url)                t(1)

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion guard
test -z "${ZBUH_INCLUDED:-}" || return 0
ZBUH_INCLUDED=1

######################################################################
# Internal: Kindle and Sentinel

zbuh_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBUH_KINDLED:-}" || return 0

  # Color support detection
  local z_use_color=0
  if test -z "${NO_COLOR:-}" && test -n "${TERM:-}" && test "${TERM}" != "dumb"; then
    z_use_color=1
  fi

  if test "${z_use_color}" = "1"; then
    readonly ZBUH_R="\033[0m"          # Reset
    readonly ZBUH_T=""                 # Text (default - no color change)
    readonly ZBUH_C="\033[36m"         # Command (cyan)
    readonly ZBUH_U="\033[35m"         # UI element (magenta)
    readonly ZBUH_W="\033[1;33m"       # Warning (bright yellow)
    readonly ZBUH_E="\033[1;31m"       # Error (bright red)
    readonly ZBUH_S="\033[1;37m"       # Section (bright white)
    readonly ZBUH_L="\033[34;4m"       # Link (blue + underline)
  else
    readonly ZBUH_R=""
    readonly ZBUH_T=""
    readonly ZBUH_C=""
    readonly ZBUH_U=""
    readonly ZBUH_W=""
    readonly ZBUH_E=""
    readonly ZBUH_S=""
    readonly ZBUH_L=""
  fi

  # Mutable kindle state — step counters, body indent, step format
  z_buh_step1_n=0
  z_buh_step2_n=0
  z_buh_body_indent=""
  z_buh_step_prefix=""
  z_buh_step_separator=". "

  readonly ZBUH_KINDLED=1
}

zbuh_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBUH_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || { zbuh_kindle; }
}

######################################################################
# Internal: Core output

zbuh_show() {
  zbuh_sentinel
  printf '%b\n' "${z_buh_body_indent}${1:-}" >&2
}

######################################################################
# Internal: Link fragment builder
#
# Sets ZBUH_LINK_FRAG to a formatted link fragment for embedding in
# combinator output.  Respects BURD_NO_HYPERLINKS fallback.
# Args: display_text url

zbuh_link_fragment() {
  zbuh_sentinel
  local -r z_text="${1:-}"
  local -r z_url="${2:-}"
  if test -n "${BURD_NO_HYPERLINKS:-}"; then
    ZBUH_LINK_FRAG="${ZBUH_L}${z_text}${ZBUH_R} <${z_url}>"
  else
    ZBUH_LINK_FRAG="${ZBUH_L}\033]8;;${z_url}\033\\\\${z_text}\033]8;;\033\\\\${ZBUH_R}"
  fi
}

######################################################################
# Public: Section headers and numbered steps

buh_section() { zbuh_sentinel; z_buh_body_indent=""; buyf_format_yawp "${BUYC_BRIGHT_WHITE}" "${1:-}"; printf '%b\n' "${z_buym_format}" >&2; }
buh_e()       { echo "" >&2; }

buh_step_style() {
  zbuh_sentinel
  z_buh_step_prefix="${1:-}"
  z_buh_step_separator="${2:-". "}"
}

buh_step1() {
  zbuh_sentinel
  z_buh_step1_n=$((z_buh_step1_n + 1))
  z_buh_step2_n=0
  z_buh_body_indent="   "
  buyf_format_yawp "${BUYC_BRIGHT_WHITE}" "${1:-}"
  printf '%b\n' "${ZBUH_S}${z_buh_step_prefix}${z_buh_step1_n}${z_buh_step_separator}${z_buym_format}" >&2
}

buh_step2() {
  zbuh_sentinel
  z_buh_step2_n=$((z_buh_step2_n + 1))
  z_buh_body_indent="      "
  buyf_format_yawp "${BUYC_BRIGHT_WHITE}" "${1:-}"
  printf '%b\n' "   ${ZBUH_S}${z_buh_step_prefix}${z_buh_step1_n}.${z_buh_step2_n}${z_buh_step_separator}${z_buym_format}" >&2
}

150	
151	######################################################################
152	# Public: Text combinators
153	#
154	# Naming: sequence of t/c/u/W/E describes positional arg colors
155	# Each letter consumes one positional argument
156	
157	# Single element (sorted)
158	buh_c()       { zbuh_show "${ZBUH_C}${1}${ZBUH_R}"; }
159	buh_E()       { zbuh_show "${ZBUH_E}${1}${ZBUH_R}"; }
160	buh_t()       { zbuh_show "${1}"; }
161	buh_u()       { zbuh_show "${ZBUH_U}${1}${ZBUH_R}"; }
162	buh_W()       { zbuh_show "${ZBUH_W}${1}${ZBUH_R}"; }
163	
164	# Two elements (sorted)
165	buh_ct()      { zbuh_show "${ZBUH_C}${1}${ZBUH_R}${2}"; }
166	buh_tc()      { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_C}${2}${ZBUH_R}"; }
167	buh_tE()      { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_E}${2}${ZBUH_R}"; }
168	buh_tu()      { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_U}${2}${ZBUH_R}"; }
169	buh_tW()      { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_W}${2}${ZBUH_R}"; }
170	buh_ut()      { zbuh_show "${ZBUH_U}${1}${ZBUH_R}${2}"; }
171	
172	# Three elements (sorted)
173	buh_tct()     { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_C}${2}${ZBUH_R}${3}"; }
174	buh_tcu()     { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_C}${2}${ZBUH_R}${ZBUH_U}${3}${ZBUH_R}"; }
175	buh_tEt()     { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_E}${2}${ZBUH_R}${3}"; }
176	buh_tuc()     { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_U}${2}${ZBUH_R}${ZBUH_C}${3}${ZBUH_R}"; }
177	buh_tut()     { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_U}${2}${ZBUH_R}${3}"; }
178	buh_tWt()     { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_W}${2}${ZBUH_R}${3}"; }
179	
180	# Four elements (sorted)
181	buh_tctc()    { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_C}${2}${ZBUH_R}${3}${ZBUH_C}${4}${ZBUH_R}"; }
182	buh_tctu()    { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_C}${2}${ZBUH_R}${3}${ZBUH_U}${4}${ZBUH_R}"; }
183	buh_tcut()    { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_C}${2}${ZBUH_R}${ZBUH_U}${3}${ZBUH_R}${4}"; }
184	buh_tuct()    { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_U}${2}${ZBUH_R}${ZBUH_C}${3}${ZBUH_R}${4}"; }
185	buh_tutE()    { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_U}${2}${ZBUH_R}${3}${ZBUH_E}${4}${ZBUH_R}"; }
186	buh_tutu()    { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_U}${2}${ZBUH_R}${3}${ZBUH_U}${4}${ZBUH_R}"; }
187	
188	# Five elements (sorted)
189	buh_tctct()   { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_C}${2}${ZBUH_R}${3}${ZBUH_C}${4}${ZBUH_R}${5}"; }
190	buh_tutut()   { zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_U}${2}${ZBUH_R}${3}${ZBUH_U}${4}${ZBUH_R}${5}"; }
191	
192	######################################################################
193	# Public: Hyperlinks
194	#
195	# buh_link "prefix text" "link text" "url" "suffix text"
196	# Renders clickable hyperlink with OSC-8 escape sequences
197	
198	buh_link() {
199	  zbuh_sentinel
200	  local z_prefix="${1:-}"
201	  local z_text="${2:-}"
202	  local z_url="${3:-}"
203	  local z_suffix="${4:-}"
204	
205	  # Blue + underline style
206	  local z_link_style="\033[34;4m"
207	
208	  if test -n "${BURD_NO_HYPERLINKS:-}"; then
209	    # Fallback: styled text with URL in angle brackets
210	    printf '%s%s%b%s%b <%s>%s\n' \
211	      "${z_buh_body_indent}" "${z_prefix}" "${z_link_style}" "${z_text}" "${ZBUH_R}" "${z_url}" "${z_suffix}" >&2
212	  else
213	    # OSC-8 hyperlink with styling
214	    printf '%s%s%b\033]8;;%s\033\\%s\033]8;;\033\\%b%s\n' \
215	      "${z_buh_body_indent}" "${z_prefix}" "${z_link_style}" "${z_url}" "${z_text}" "${ZBUH_R}" "${z_suffix}" >&2
216	  fi
217	}
218	
219	######################################################################
220	# Public: Link combinators
221	#
222	# The 'l' letter consumes TWO positional args (display_text, url) and
223	# renders an OSC-8 terminal hyperlink.  All other letters consume one.
224	#
225	# Arg mapping example for buh_tlt:
226	#   buh_tlt  "A "  "hallmark"  "https://...#hallmark"  " is a named artifact."
227	#            ^^^^  ^^^^^^^^^^  ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^   ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
228	#            t(1)  l(1:text)   l(2:url)                t(1)
229	
230	buh_lt()      { zbuh_link_fragment "${1}" "${2}"; zbuh_show "${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}${3}"; }
231	buh_tl()      { zbuh_link_fragment "${2}" "${3}"; zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}"; }
232	buh_tlt()     { zbuh_link_fragment "${2}" "${3}"; zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}${4}"; }
233	buh_tlc()     { zbuh_link_fragment "${2}" "${3}"; zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}${ZBUH_C}${4}${ZBUH_R}"; }
234	
235	buh_tltlt() {
236	  zbuh_link_fragment "${2}" "${3}"
237	  local -r z_frag1="${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}"
238	  zbuh_link_fragment "${5}" "${6}"
239	  zbuh_show "${1}${z_frag1}${4}${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}${7}"
240	}
241	
242	buh_tltltlt() {
243	  zbuh_link_fragment "${2}" "${3}"
244	  local -r z_frag1="${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}"
245	  zbuh_link_fragment "${5}" "${6}"
246	  local -r z_frag2="${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}"
247	  zbuh_link_fragment "${8}" "${9}"
248	  zbuh_show "${1}${z_frag1}${4}${z_frag2}${7}${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}${10}"
249	}
250	
251	buh_tltltltlt() {
252	  zbuh_link_fragment "${2}" "${3}"
253	  local -r z_frag1="${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}"
254	  zbuh_link_fragment "${5}" "${6}"
255	  local -r z_frag2="${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}"
256	  zbuh_link_fragment "${8}" "${9}"
257	  local -r z_frag3="${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}"
258	  zbuh_link_fragment "${11}" "${12}"
259	  zbuh_show "${1}${z_frag1}${4}${z_frag2}${7}${z_frag3}${10}${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}${13}"
260	}
261	
262	buh_tltTtT() {
263	  zbuh_link_fragment "${2}" "${3}"
264	  local -r z_frag1="${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}"
265	  zbuh_tabtarget_fragment "${5}"
266	  local -r z_frag2="${ZBUH_TT_FRAG}"
267	  zbuh_tabtarget_fragment "${7}"
268	  zbuh_show "${1}${z_frag1}${4}${z_frag2}${6}${ZBUH_TT_FRAG}${8}"
269	}
270	
271	######################################################################
272	# Internal: Tabtarget fragment builder
273	#
274	# Sets ZBUH_TT_FRAG to a resolved tabtarget path in cyan for embedding
275	# in combinator output.  Resolves colophon via glob.
276	# Args: colophon
277	
278	zbuh_tabtarget_fragment() {
279	  zbuh_sentinel
280	  local z_matches=("${BURD_TABTARGET_DIR}"/${1}.*)
281	  test -e "${z_matches[0]}" || buc_die "buh: no tabtarget for colophon '${1}'"
282	  ZBUH_TT_FRAG="${ZBUH_C}${z_matches[0]}${ZBUH_R}"
283	}
284	
285	# Imprint tabtarget fragment: resolves colophon + imprint to a specific
286	# imprint tabtarget.  Glob: ${colophon}.*.${imprint}.sh
287	# Args: colophon imprint
288	
289	zbuh_imprint_fragment() {
290	  zbuh_sentinel
291	  local z_matches=("${BURD_TABTARGET_DIR}"/${1}.*.${2}.sh)
292	  test -e "${z_matches[0]}" || buc_die "buh: no tabtarget for colophon '${1}' imprint '${2}'"
293	  ZBUH_TT_FRAG="${ZBUH_C}${z_matches[0]}${ZBUH_R}"
294	}
295	
296	######################################################################
297	# Public: Tabtarget combinators
298	#
299	# T consumes one positional arg (colophon), resolves to tabtarget path,
300	# renders in cyan.  Parallel to l (link) combinator.
301	# I consumes two positional args (colophon, imprint), resolves to a
302	# specific imprint tabtarget.
303	# Trailing c after T or I appends cyan text (arguments) to the resolved
304	# path, keeping the whole command visually unified.
305	
306	buh_T()       { zbuh_tabtarget_fragment "${1}"; zbuh_show "${ZBUH_TT_FRAG}"; }
307	buh_tT()      { zbuh_tabtarget_fragment "${2}"; zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_TT_FRAG}"; }
308	buh_tTc()     { zbuh_tabtarget_fragment "${2}"; zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_TT_FRAG}${ZBUH_C}${3}${ZBUH_R}"; }
309	buh_tI()      { zbuh_imprint_fragment "${2}" "${3}"; zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_TT_FRAG}"; }
310	buh_tIc()     { zbuh_imprint_fragment "${2}" "${3}"; zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_TT_FRAG}${ZBUH_C}${4}${ZBUH_R}"; }
311	buh_cT()      { zbuh_tabtarget_fragment "${2}"; zbuh_show "${ZBUH_C}${1}${ZBUH_R}${ZBUH_TT_FRAG}"; }
312	buh_tlT()     { zbuh_link_fragment "${2}" "${3}"; zbuh_tabtarget_fragment "${4}"; zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG} ${ZBUH_TT_FRAG}"; }
313	buh_tltT()    { zbuh_link_fragment "${2}" "${3}"; zbuh_tabtarget_fragment "${5}"; zbuh_show "${1}${ZBUH_LINK_FRAG}${4}${ZBUH_TT_FRAG}"; }
314	
315	######################################################################
316	# Public: Yelp-aware tabtarget display
317	#
318	# buh_tt prefix colophon [imprint] [args]
319	#   Resolves tabtarget via yawp, displays with prefix.
320	#   Replaces buh_tT, buh_tTc, buh_tI, buh_tIc combinators.
321	
322	buh_tt() {
323	  zbuh_sentinel
324	  buyy_tt_yawp "${2:-}" "${3:-}" "${4:-}"
325	  local -r z_tt="${z_buym_yelp}"
326	  buh_line "${1:-}${z_tt}"
327	}
328	
329	######################################################################
330	# Public: Index renderers
331	#
332	# Group-level handbook indexes shared across kits.
333	# Each function renders one kit's handbook group tops.
334	
335	buh_index_buk() {
336	  buh_section  "Generic OS Procedures (Bash Utility Kit)"
337	  buh_t        "  Project-independent OS-level mechanisms."
338	  buh_tT       "  Windows:  " "buw-hw"
339	}
340	
341	######################################################################
342	# Public: Pre-composed line output
343	#
344	# buh_line string
345	#   Indent-aware (respects z_buh_body_indent from step context).
346	#   Designed for lines pre-composed from yelp capture fragments
347	#   via direct bash variable interpolation:
348	#     buh_line "A ${RBYC_DEPOT} is where images live."

348	#     buh_line "A ${RBYC_DEPOT} is where images live."
349	#   Yelps are pre-rendered (literal ESC bytes) and must not contain %.
350	
351	buh_line() {
352	  zbuh_sentinel
353	  buyf_format_yawp "${BUYC_RESET}" "${1:-}"
354	  printf '%b\n' "${z_buh_body_indent}${z_buym_format}" >&2
355	}
356	
357	######################################################################
358	# Public: Semantic line functions
359	#
360	# Each routes through buyf_format_yawp with a BUYC_* ambient color,
361	# then prints via indent-aware printf.  Diastema markers in the string
362	# are resolved to ANSI/OSC-8 at display time.
363	#
364	# buh_line   — prose default (BUYC_RESET ambient)
365	# buh_code   — command/code (BUYC_CYAN ambient)
366	# buh_warn   — warning (BUYC_BRIGHT_YELLOW ambient)
367	# buh_error  — error (BUYC_BRIGHT_RED ambient)
368	
369	buh_code() {
370	  zbuh_sentinel
371	  buyf_format_yawp "${BUYC_CYAN}" "${1:-}"
372	  printf '%b\n' "${z_buh_body_indent}${z_buym_format}" >&2
373	}
374	
375	buh_warn() {
376	  zbuh_sentinel
377	  buyf_format_yawp "${BUYC_BRIGHT_YELLOW}" "${1:-}"
378	  printf '%b\n' "${z_buh_body_indent}${z_buym_format}" >&2
379	}
380	
381	buh_error() {
382	  zbuh_sentinel
383	  buyf_format_yawp "${BUYC_BRIGHT_RED}" "${1:-}"
384	  printf '%b\n' "${z_buh_body_indent}${z_buym_format}" >&2
385	}
386	
387	######################################################################
388	# Public: User prompts
389	
390	# buh_prompt "prompt text"
391	# Displays prompt and reads user input, returns via stdout
392	buh_prompt() {
393	  zbuh_sentinel
394	  printf '%s' "${1:-}" >&2
395	  local z_input
396	  read -r z_input
397	  printf '%s' "${z_input}"
398	}
399	
400	# buh_prompt_required "prompt text" "error message"
401	# Like buh_prompt but dies if input is empty
402	buh_prompt_required() {
403	  local z_input
404	  z_input=$(buh_prompt "${1:-}")
405	  if test -z "${z_input}"; then
406	    zbuh_show "${ZBUH_E}ERROR:${ZBUH_R} ${2:-Input required}"
407	    return 1

#     buh_line "A ${RBYC_DEPOT} is where images live."
#   Yelps are pre-rendered (literal ESC bytes) and must not contain %.

buh_line() {
  zbuh_sentinel
  buyf_format_yawp "${BUYC_RESET}" "${1:-}"
  printf '%b\n' "${z_buh_body_indent}${z_buym_format}" >&2
}

######################################################################
# Public: Semantic line functions
#
# Each routes through buyf_format_yawp with a BUYC_* ambient color,
# then prints via indent-aware printf.  Diastema markers in the string
# are resolved to ANSI/OSC-8 at display time.
#
# buh_line   — prose default (BUYC_RESET ambient)
# buh_code   — command/code (BUYC_CYAN ambient)
# buh_warn   — warning (BUYC_BRIGHT_YELLOW ambient)
# buh_error  — error (BUYC_BRIGHT_RED ambient)

buh_code() {
  zbuh_sentinel
  buyf_format_yawp "${BUYC_CYAN}" "${1:-}"
  printf '%b\n' "${z_buh_body_indent}${z_buym_format}" >&2
}

buh_warn() {
  zbuh_sentinel
  buyf_format_yawp "${BUYC_BRIGHT_YELLOW}" "${1:-}"
  printf '%b\n' "${z_buh_body_indent}${z_buym_format}" >&2
}

buh_error() {
  zbuh_sentinel
  buyf_format_yawp "${BUYC_BRIGHT_RED}" "${1:-}"
  printf '%b\n' "${z_buh_body_indent}${z_buym_format}" >&2
}

######################################################################
# Public: User prompts

# buh_prompt "prompt text"
# Displays prompt and reads user input, returns via stdout
buh_prompt() {
  zbuh_sentinel
  printf '%s' "${1:-}" >&2
  local z_input
  read -r z_input
  printf '%s' "${z_input}"
}

# buh_prompt_required "prompt text" "error message"
# Like buh_prompt but dies if input is empty
buh_prompt_required() {
  local z_input
  z_input=$(buh_prompt "${1:-}")
  if test -z "${z_input}"; then
    zbuh_show "${ZBUH_E}ERROR:${ZBUH_R} ${2:-Input required}"
    return 1

######################################################################
# Yelp yawp functions (buyy_*)
#
# Pure assignment to z_buym_yelp.  No stdout, no stderr, cannot fail.
# Caller MUST capture to local -r immediately — next yawp overwrites.
# See module header for usage pattern, BCG for full contract.

# buyy_cmd_yawp text — stamps CMD region
# → local -r z_cmd="${z_buym_yelp}"
buyy_cmd_yawp() {
  zbuym_sentinel
  z_buym_yelp="${ZBUYM_DIASTEMA_CMD}${1:-}${ZBUYM_DIASTEMA_END}"
}

# buyy_ui_yawp text — stamps UI region
# → local -r z_ui="${z_buym_yelp}"
buyy_ui_yawp() {
  zbuym_sentinel
  z_buym_yelp="${ZBUYM_DIASTEMA_UI}${1:-}${ZBUYM_DIASTEMA_END}"
}

# buyy_href_yawp url display — stamps HREF region with raw URL
# → local -r z_href="${z_buym_yelp}"
buyy_href_yawp() {
  zbuym_sentinel
  z_buym_yelp="${ZBUYM_DIASTEMA_HREF_URL}${1:-}${ZBUYM_DIASTEMA_HREF_TEXT}${2:-}${ZBUYM_DIASTEMA_END}"
}

# buyy_link_yawp base_url anchor [display] — stamps LINK region
# → local -r z_link="${z_buym_yelp}"
buyy_link_yawp() {
  zbuym_sentinel
  local -r z_url="${1:-}#${2:-}"
  local -r z_display="${3:-${2:-}}"
  z_buym_yelp="${ZBUYM_DIASTEMA_LINK_URL}${z_url}${ZBUYM_DIASTEMA_LINK_TEXT}${z_display}${ZBUYM_DIASTEMA_END}"
}

# buyy_tt_yawp colophon [imprint] [args] — resolves tabtarget, stamps TT region
# → local -r z_tt="${z_buym_yelp}"
buyy_tt_yawp() {
  zbuym_sentinel
  local -r z_colophon="${1:-}"
  local z_path=""
  if test -n "${2:-}"; then
    local z_matches=("${BURD_TABTARGET_DIR}"/${z_colophon}.*.${2}.sh)
    test -e "${z_matches[0]}" && z_path="${z_matches[0]}" || z_path="??${z_colophon}.${2}??"
  else
    local z_matches=("${BURD_TABTARGET_DIR}"/${z_colophon}.*)
    test -e "${z_matches[0]}" && z_path="${z_matches[0]}" || z_path="??${z_colophon}??"
  fi
  z_buym_yelp="${ZBUYM_DIASTEMA_TT}${z_path}${3:-}${ZBUYM_DIASTEMA_END}"
}

######################################################################
# Format yawp (buyf_*)
#
# buyf_format_yawp color string
#   The single intelligence point.  Takes a BUYC_* color constant
#   (the line's ambient color) and a diastema-marked string.
#   Sets z_buym_format with the resolved string.
#
# Resolution:
#   1. Simple markers (CMD, UI, TT) → corresponding BUYC_* color
#   2. Structured markers (HREF, LINK) → OSC-8 or fallback
#   3. All DIASTEMA_END → ambient color
#   4. Prepend ambient, append BUYC_RESET

buyf_format_yawp() {
  zbuym_sentinel
  local z_ambient="${1:-}"
  local z_str="${2:-}"

  # If string has no diastema markers, fast path
  case "${z_str}" in
    *$'\x02'*) ;;
    *)
      z_buym_format="${z_ambient}${z_str}${BUYC_RESET}"
      return 0
      ;;
  esac

  # --- Resolve structured markers first (HREF and LINK) ---
  # These have URL data between opener and text marker, so they need
  # regex extraction before simple replacement can work.

  # Process HREF markers
  local z_href_pattern="${ZBUYM_DIASTEMA_HREF_URL}([^${ZBUYM_DIASTEMA_HREF_TEXT}]*)${ZBUYM_DIASTEMA_HREF_TEXT}([^${ZBUYM_DIASTEMA_END}]*)${ZBUYM_DIASTEMA_END}"
  while [[ "${z_str}" =~ ${z_href_pattern} ]]; do
    local z_href_url="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
    local z_href_text="${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"
    local z_href_full="${BASH_REMATCH[0]}"
    local z_href_replacement=""
    if test "${ZBUYM_USE_HYPERLINKS}" = "1"; then
      z_href_replacement="${BUYC_HREF}\033]8;;${z_href_url}\033\\\\${z_href_text}\033]8;;\033\\\\${z_ambient}"
    elif test -n "${BUYC_HREF}"; then
      z_href_replacement="${BUYC_HREF}${z_href_text}${z_ambient} <${z_href_url}>"
    else
      z_href_replacement="${z_href_text}"
    fi

content.text:
Good. I now have the full picture of the conversion pattern. The key mappings are:

- `buh_t "text"` → `buh_line "text"` (for lines with yelp fragments) or stays `buh_t` for plain text with no yelp content
- `buh_c "text"` → `buh_code "text"` 
- `buh_E "text"` → `buh_error "text"`
- `buh_tW "" "text"` → `buh_warn "text"`
- `buh_ct " [*] " "text"` → `buh_line " [*] ${z_cmd_yelp_fragment}text"`  (need to use yelp captures)
- `buh_tc "prefix" "code"` → need to use `buyy_cmd_yawp` + capture + `buh_line`
- `buh_tct` → yawp + capture + `buh_line`
- `buh_tT` → `buh_tt`
- `buh_tTc` → `buh_tt` with args
- `buh_tI` → `buh_tt` with imprint
- `buh_tE` → `buh_line` with error yelp or restructured

Actually wait — looking at the docket more carefully. The old combinators in `rbho_first_crucible` that were NOT yet converted are specifically: `buh_t`, `buh_c`, `buh_E`, `buh_tW`, `buh_ct`, `buh_tc`, `buh_tct`, `buh_tT`, `buh_tTc`, `buh_tI`. The `buy_cmd_capture` calls were already migrated.

Let me look at which of these `buh_t` calls are plain text (no yelp content, can stay as-is or become `buh_line`) vs which actually need conversion.

Looking at the "before" output, it renders correctly. The conversion is about moving from the old combinator API to the new yelp-aware API. Let me understand the distinction:

- `buh_t` stays for truly plain text with no diastema markers
- `buh_line` is for text that MAY contain diastema markers (yelp fragments). It wraps in `buyf_format_yawp` so diastema markers are resolved
- `buh_c` → `buh_code` (routes through `buyf_format_yawp`)
- `buh_E` → `buh_error`
- `buh_tW "" "warning text"` → `buh_warn "warning text"`
- Old 2+ element combinators (`buh_ct`, `buh_tc`, `buh_tct`, `buh_tE`) need to be decomposed into yawp captures + `buh_line`
- `buh_tT`/`buh_tTc`/`buh_tI` → `buh_tt`

Let me now do the conversion. The function spans lines 798-1165. I'll work through it section by section.

# --- Header ---
  buh_section "Start a Crucible Using Local Builds"
  buh_e
  buh_line "A ${RBYC_CRUCIBLE} is a sandboxed container environment with enforced"
  buh_t   "network isolation. You are going to build one on your workstation"
  buh_t   "and run Claude Code inside it — no cloud account, no credentials"
  buh_t   "beyond your own Claude subscription."
  buh_e
  buh_line "This track uses the ${RBYC_CCYOLO} ${RBYC_NAMEPLATE}: a Claude Code sandbox that can"
  buh_t   "only reach Anthropic. Everything else is blocked."
  buh_e

  # Docker gate
  if test "${z_has_docker}" = "0"; then
    buh_E   "Docker is not available on this machine."
    buh_t   "Install Docker Desktop (or dockerd in WSL) and re-run this handbook."
    buh_e
    buh_tT  "Return to start: " "${RBZ_ONBOARD_START_HERE}"
    buh_e
    return 0
  fi

  buh_t   "Prerequisite: a Claude OAuth subscription (you will authenticate"
  buh_t   "inside the container via copy/paste from your browser)."
  buh_e

  buh_step_style "Step " " — "

  # =================================================================
  # Step 1: Build images locally
  # =================================================================
  buh_step1 "Build images locally"
  buh_e
  buh_line "A ${RBYC_VESSEL} is a specification for a container image — a Dockerfile"
  buh_line "and build context. A ${RBYC_HALLMARK} is a specific build instance,"
  buh_t   "identified by a timestamp tag."
  buh_e
  buh_line "${RBYC_KLUDGE} builds a vessel image locally using Docker — no cloud, no"
  buh_t   "registry, no credentials. The fastest path from Dockerfile to running"
  buh_t   "container without cloud image backup."
  buh_e

  # --- Substep 1a: Kludge the sentry ---
  buh_step2 "Kludge the sentry"
  buh_e
  buh_line "The ${RBYC_SENTRY} is the gatekeeper container. It runs iptables"
  buh_t   "and dnsmasq to enforce network policy — only domains you whitelist"
  buh_t   "are reachable from inside."
  buh_e
  buh_t   "Kludge it:"
  buh_e
  buh_tTc "   " "${RBZ_KLUDGE_VESSEL}" " ${z_sentry_vessel}"
  buh_e
  buh_t   "The output prints a hallmark tag — something like"
  buh_tc  "" "k260413155458-f9dcb6d9"
  buh_t   "Copy it. You will paste it into the nameplate in Step 2."
  buh_e

  # Sentry kludge image probe
  if test "${z_sentry_image_exists}" = "1"; then
    buh_ct  " [*] " "Kludge-tagged sentry image found locally"
  else
    buh_t   " [ ] No kludge-tagged sentry image found"
  fi
  buh_e

  buh_tW  "" "Kludge requires a clean git tree."
  buh_t   "If you have uncommitted changes, commit them first.  This is by"
  buh_t   "design — container images not backed by git commits easy to confuse."
  buh_e

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Bash Utility Yelp Module — Diastema wire format with yawp functions
#
# Yelp yawp functions are inert marker-stampers.  They embed non-printing
# diastema byte markers via pure string assignment into a shared group
# return variable (z_buym_yelp).  No subshells, no stdout, no stderr,
# cannot fail.
#
# All terminal capability decisions are deferred to buyf_format_yawp,
# which resolves diastema markers into ANSI color sequences and OSC-8
# hyperlinks at display time.
#
# Namespace:
#   buyy_  — yelp yawp functions (set z_buym_yelp)
#   buyc_  — configurators (set ZBUYM_CONFIG_MODE before kindle)
#   buyf_  — format yawp (set z_buym_format)
#   buym_  — module infrastructure (kindle, sentinel)
#
# Usage pattern — yawp then IMMEDIATELY capture to local -r:
#   buyy_cmd_yawp "git status"
#   local -r z_cmd="${z_buym_yelp}"        # capture before next yawp
#   buyy_link_yawp "${z_docs}" "Depot"
#   local -r z_depot="${z_buym_yelp}"       # capture before next yawp
#   buh_line "Run ${z_cmd} to see your ${z_depot} status."
#
# Never use z_buym_yelp directly in buh_line — it is a transient
# return slot, not a durable value.  See buyy_* section for details.

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion guard
test -z "${ZBUYM_SOURCED:-}" || return 0
ZBUYM_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Configurators (buyc_*)
#
# Called before kindle.  Each sets ZBUYM_CONFIG_MODE flag.
# Kindle reads the mode and defines readonly BUYC_* palette.

ZBUYM_CONFIG_MODE="dispatch"

buyc_dispatch()      { ZBUYM_CONFIG_MODE="dispatch"; }
buyc_unconditional() { ZBUYM_CONFIG_MODE="unconditional"; }
buyc_plain()         { ZBUYM_CONFIG_MODE="plain"; }

######################################################################
# Module kindle — defines all constants and initializes mutable state

zbuym_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBUYM_KINDLED:-}" || return 0

  local z_use_color=0

  case "${ZBUYM_CONFIG_MODE}" in
    unconditional)
      z_use_color=1
      ;;
    plain)
      z_use_color=0
      ;;
    dispatch|*)
      if test -z "${NO_COLOR:-}" && test -n "${TERM:-}" && test "${TERM}" != "dumb"; then
        z_use_color=1
      fi
      ;;
  esac

  # --- Public color constants (BUYC_*) ---
  if test "${z_use_color}" = "1"; then
    readonly BUYC_RESET="\033[0m"
    readonly BUYC_CYAN="\033[36m"
    readonly BUYC_MAGENTA="\033[35m"
    readonly BUYC_BRIGHT_YELLOW="\033[1;33m"
    readonly BUYC_BRIGHT_RED="\033[1;31m"
    readonly BUYC_BRIGHT_WHITE="\033[1;37m"
    readonly BUYC_LINK="\033[34;4m"
    readonly BUYC_HREF="\033[34;4m"
  else
    readonly BUYC_RESET=""
    readonly BUYC_CYAN=""
    readonly BUYC_MAGENTA=""
    readonly BUYC_BRIGHT_YELLOW=""
    readonly BUYC_BRIGHT_RED=""
    readonly BUYC_BRIGHT_WHITE=""
    readonly BUYC_LINK=""
    readonly BUYC_HREF=""
  fi

  # --- Hyperlink mode ---
  local z_hyperlinks=0
  if test "${z_use_color}" = "1" && test -z "${BURD_NO_HYPERLINKS:-}"; then
    z_hyperlinks=1
  fi
  readonly ZBUYM_USE_HYPERLINKS="${z_hyperlinks}"

  # --- Diastema markers (non-printing byte sequences) ---
  # Each marker is a unique non-printing sequence that yelp yawp functions
  # stamp into strings.  buyf_format_yawp resolves them at display time.
  # Prefix byte is \x02 (STX) — \x01 (SOH) is reserved by bash internally
  # for BASH_REMATCH group delimiting and gets silently dropped from matches.
  readonly ZBUYM_DIASTEMA_CMD=$'\x02\x11'
  readonly ZBUYM_DIASTEMA_UI=$'\x02\x12'
  readonly ZBUYM_DIASTEMA_HREF_URL=$'\x02\x13'
  readonly ZBUYM_DIASTEMA_HREF_TEXT=$'\x02\x14'
  readonly ZBUYM_DIASTEMA_LINK_URL=$'\x02\x15'
  readonly ZBUYM_DIASTEMA_LINK_TEXT=$'\x02\x16'
  readonly ZBUYM_DIASTEMA_TT=$'\x02\x17'
  readonly ZBUYM_DIASTEMA_END=$'\x02\x18'

  # --- Mutable kindle state for yawp groups ---
  z_buym_yelp=""
  z_buym_format=""

  # --- Legacy capture compatibility constants ---
  # buy_yelp.sh consumers (rbyc_common.sh) still use capture functions.
  # These constants replicate ZBUY_* so the old API continues to work
  # until rbyc migrates to yawp.
  readonly ZBUY_R="${BUYC_RESET}"
  readonly ZBUY_C="${BUYC_CYAN}"
  readonly ZBUY_U="${BUYC_MAGENTA}"
  readonly ZBUY_L="${BUYC_LINK}"
  readonly ZBUY_USE_HYPERLINKS="${z_use_color}"
  if test -n "${BURD_NO_HYPERLINKS:-}"; then
    readonly ZBUY_NO_HYPERLINKS=1
  else
    readonly ZBUY_NO_HYPERLINKS=0
  fi
  readonly ZBUY_KINDLED=1

  readonly ZBUYM_KINDLED=1
}

zbuym_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBUYM_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || { zbuym_kindle; }
}

# Legacy sentinel alias — rbyc_common.sh calls zbuy_sentinel
zbuy_sentinel() { zbuym_sentinel; }

######################################################################
# Legacy capture functions
#
# These replicate buy_yelp.sh's public API so existing consumers
# (rbyc_common.sh) continue to work without modification.
# They produce stdout for $() capture — the old pattern.

buy_link_capture() {
  zbuym_sentinel
  local -r z_base="${1:-}"
  local -r z_anchor="${2:-}"
  local -r z_display="${3:-${z_anchor}}"
  local -r z_url="${z_base}#${z_anchor}"

  if test "${ZBUY_USE_HYPERLINKS}" = "1" && test "${ZBUY_NO_HYPERLINKS}" = "0"; then
    printf '%b' "${ZBUY_L}\033]8;;${z_url}\033\\\\${z_display}\033]8;;\033\\\\${ZBUY_R}"
  elif test "${ZBUY_USE_HYPERLINKS}" = "1"; then
    printf '%b' "${ZBUY_L}${z_display}${ZBUY_R} <${z_url}>"
  else
    printf '%s' "${z_display}"
  fi
}

buy_cmd_capture() {
  zbuym_sentinel
  local -r z_text="${1:-}"
  if test -n "${ZBUY_C}"; then
    printf '%b' "${ZBUY_C}${z_text}${ZBUY_R}"
  else
    printf '%s' "${z_text}"
  fi
}

buy_ui_capture() {
  zbuym_sentinel
  local -r z_text="${1:-}"
  if test -n "${ZBUY_U}"; then
    printf '%b' "${ZBUY_U}${z_text}${ZBUY_R}"
  else
    printf '%s' "${z_text}"
  fi
}

buy_tt_capture() {
  zbuym_sentinel
  local -r z_colophon="${1:-}"
  local z_matches=("${BURD_TABTARGET_DIR}"/${z_colophon}.*)
  test -e "${z_matches[0]}" || { printf '%s' "??${z_colophon}??"; return 0; }
  if test -n "${ZBUY_C}"; then
    printf '%b' "${ZBUY_C}${z_matches[0]}${ZBUY_R}"
  else
    printf '%s' "${z_matches[0]}"
  fi
}

# Legacy configurator aliases
buy_configure_dispatch()      { buyc_dispatch; }
buy_configure_unconditional() { buyc_unconditional; }
buy_configure_plain()         { buyc_plain; }

# Legacy kindle alias
zbuy_kindle() { zbuym_kindle; }

zrbho_po_status() {
  local -r z_flag="${1:-}"
  local -r z_text="${2:-}"
  if test "${z_flag}" = "1"; then
    buyy_cmd_yawp " [*] "
    local -r z_mark="${z_buym_yelp}"
    buh_line "${z_mark}${z_text}"
  else
    buh_line " [ ] ${z_text}"
  fi
}

# Extract a KEY=VALUE from a file; stdout empty if missing.  No sourcing.
zrbho_po_extract_capture() {
  local -r z_file="${1:-}"
  local -r z_key="${2:-}"
  test -n "${z_key}"  || return 1
  test -f "${z_file}" || return 1
  local z_line=""
  while IFS= read -r z_line; do
    case "${z_line}" in "${z_key}="*) echo "${z_line#"${z_key}="}"; return 0 ;; esac
  done < "${z_file}"
  return 1
}

######################################################################
# Shared credential probe — sets caller-scoped:
# z_has_payor, z_has_governor, z_has_director, z_has_retriever, z_secrets_dir
